overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/secure-transport-result-codes].

## Discussion

If you previously called the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetPeerDomainName(_:_:_:)] function to specify a fully qualified domain name for the peer certificate, you can use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLGetPeerDomainName(_:_:_:)] function to retrieve the peer domain name. Before doing so, you must call the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLGetPeerDomainNameLength(_:_:)] function to retrieve the buffer size needed for the domain name.

# SSLGetPeerDomainNameLength(_:_:)

**Function**

Determines the length of a previously set peer domain name.

## Declaration

```swift
func SSLGetPeerDomainNameLength(_ context: SSLContext, _ peerNameLen: UnsafeMutablePointer<Int>) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **context**: An SSL session context reference.
- **peerNameLen**: On return, points to the length of the peer domain name.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/secure-transport-result-codes].

## Discussion

If you previously called the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetPeerDomainName(_:_:_:)] function to specify a fully qualified domain name for the peer certificate, you can use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLGetPeerDomainName(_:_:_:)] function to retrieve the peer domain name. Before doing so, you must call the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLGetPeerDomainNameLength(_:_:)] function to retrieve the buffer size needed for the domain name.
title: SSLGetPeerDomainNameLength(_:_:)
